items.headline: Four-unit condo complex permitted in Venture City
items.summary: A 4-unit multifamily building at 31 Isabela Tr receives a permit, one of the few new housing projects in this batch beyond single-unit homes and ADUs. The $1.2M project suggests mid-scale infill development in the Venture City area.

items.headline: Two detached ADUs permitted at single North 2nd Street lot
items.summary: Two separate detached ADUs are permitted at 795 N 2nd St, suggesting the property owner is maximizing housing density on one parcel. This pattern of multiple ADUs on one lot may signal a shift in how San José property owners are using current zoning rules.

items.headline: City Council revokes tobacco retailer licenses for two shops
items.summary: The council held public hearings and voted to revoke the business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The specific violations or grounds for revocation were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: City to update health and safety element of general plan
items.summary: The City Council scheduled a study session to review and update the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan, which covers 2015–2040. This element addresses how the city manages risks and protects residents' wellbeing.
